1. Why use trackBy in Angular
	1. `trackBy` lets Angular identify list items by a unique key instead of object reference, preventing unnecessary DOM re-renders and improving performance, especially in large or frequently updated lists.”



**Root Cause:** Intermittent **NG0908 errors** occurred because **main.js and polyfills.js shared loadOrder=2**, so occasionally main.js bootstrapped Angular before Zone.js was registered. Our recent CSS-loading refactor shifted **download timing enough for that race condition b/w main.js and polyfills.js to surface.**

**Solution**: We bumped main.js to loadOrder=3, ensuring Zone.js always loads first.

![image](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

```json
// Array of all resources to load.

var resourcesToLoad = [

{

filename: "styles.css",

elementType: "link",

loadOrder: 1,

requiresCustomCSS: true

},

{

filename: "runtime.js",

elementType: "script",

loadOrder: 1

},

{

filename: "polyfills.js", // here zone.js exist 

elementType: "script",

loadOrder: 2

},

{

filename: "main.js",

elementType: "script",

loadOrder: 3
}
```
